heading: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission
label: 60502 Federal Energy Regulatory Commission
text: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission has the duties and powers related to the establishment of a rate or charge for the transportation of oil by pipeline or the valuation of that pipeline that were vested on , in the Interstate Commerce Commission or an officer or component of the Interstate Commerce Commission.October 1, 1977
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)6050242:7172(b)., , .Aug. 4, 1977Pub. L. 95–91, § 402(b)91 Stat. 58449:101 (note prec.)., , (2) (related to § 402(b) of Department of Energy Organization Act), .Oct. 17, 1978Pub. L. 95–473, § 4(c)(1)(B)92 Stat. 1470The words “duties and powers . . . that were vested . . . in” are coextensive with, and substituted for, “transferred to, and vested in . . . all functions and authority of” for clarity and to eliminate unnecessary words. The word “regulatory” is omitted as surplus. The words “on ” are added to reflect the effective date of the transfer of the duties and powers to the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission.October 1, 1977

Abolition of Interstate Commerce Commission and Transfer of FunctionsInterstate Commerce Commission abolished and functions of Commission transferred, except as otherwise provided in , to Surface Transportation Board effective , by , and , set out as a note under . References to Interstate Commerce Commission deemed to refer to Surface Transportation Board, a member or employee of the Board, or Secretary of Transportation, as appropriate, see , set out as a note under .Pub.
